title: Specify plan.yaml
description:
Validates the structure of `plan.yaml` (at the repo root): the change name, the optional plan-level `lifecycle` (`pending | approved` per the workflow contract §Workflow vocabulary), the optional named-sources map, and the ordered list of plan slices with their dependencies, status, and (per the workflow contract) structured source bindings plus the optional `divergence` enum. Strict schema — unknown top-level and per-slice fields are rejected.

Each slice binds a `project` (the target registry project; optional on disk — an omitted `project` resolves to the sole project in the topology) and an optional `divergence` enum (`likely | accepted | rejected`). The target adapter (`name@vN`) is no longer stored per slice — it is resolved on demand from the bound project's topology (the committed `.specify/topology.lock` for a workspace, `project.yaml.adapter` for a single regular project). `slices[].sources[]` items accept either a bare-string source key (shorthand for `{ source: <key>, lead: <slice.name> }`) or the structured `{ source, lead }` object form; the top-level `sources` map carries the structured `{ adapter, path?, value? }` object per source key — the 1.x bare-string shorthand was dropped at the 2.0 cut and exactly one of `path` or `value` is required. The per-entry status enum is the 2.0-collapsed `{pending, in-progress, done}` (v1 has no `blocked`/`failed`/`skipped`); the plan-level `lifecycle` enum is the collapsed `{pending, approved}` — `drained` and `currently executing` are computed from per-entry status at read time.

workflow §D3: each slice MAY carry an optional `authority-override` map keyed by claim kind whose values are source keys present in the slice's own `sources[]` list — orphan keys are rejected by `specify slice validate` with `slice-authority-override-orphan-source`. workflow §D5: every `divergence` value (including `likely`) is CLI-written via `specify plan amend --divergence`; the plan skill no longer patches the file directly.

name.description: Change name. Kebab-case; becomes the archive filename prefix.

sources.description: Named source bindings. Keys are kebab-case identifiers referenced by each slice's `sources` list. Values are the structured `{ adapter, path?, value? }` object — exactly one of `path` or `value` is required. The pre-2.0 bare-string shorthand was dropped at the 2.0 cut.

slices.description: Ordered list of plan slices. Order is the tie-breaker for `specify plan next` when multiple entries are eligible.

lifecycle.description: workflow plan-level lifecycle gate. `pending` is the default after `plan create`; `approved` is operator-stamped at Gate 1 via `specify plan transition <plan-name> approved`. Two stored states only — `currently executing` and `drained` are computed at read time from per-entry `status`.

kebabName.description: Kebab-case identifier: lowercase letters and digits grouped by single internal hyphens; no leading, trailing, or consecutive hyphens. Matches `specify_slice::actions::validate_name`.

claimKind.description: Closed claim-kind enum mirrored from `evidence.schema.json#/$defs/claimKind`. Kept byte-identical so the per-slice `authority-override` keys validate against the same closed set.

path.description: Filesystem path or repo location the adapter binds against. Mutually exclusive with `value`.

value.description: Literal value supplied directly to the adapter (e.g. the operator brief text for `intent`). Mutually exclusive with `path`.

sourceBinding.description: workflow §Source — a plan-level source binding. The 1.x bare-string shorthand was dropped at the 2.0 cut; every entry is now the structured object form with exactly one of `path` or `value`.

oneOf.description: Bare-string shorthand: equivalent to `{ source: <key>, lead: <slice.name> }`. Used predominantly for the degenerate `intent` case (`sources: [intent]`).

source.description: Source key matching a top-level `plan.yaml.sources.<key>` binding.

lead.description: Lead id matching a `## Lead inventory` block in `discovery.md`, resolved within the binding's `source`.

sliceSourceBinding.description: workflow §`Slice.sources` — one (source, lead) binding per contributing source.

planSlice.description: Per workflow Â§Adapter vocabulary, every slice entry binds a `project` (target registry project). `project` is optional on disk: an omitted value resolves to the sole project in the topology, so a single-regular-project plan need not repeat the project name. The target adapter is not stored on the slice â€” it is resolved from the bound project's topology (`registry.yaml` or `project.yaml.adapter`).

name.description: Slice name. Unique across the plan; becomes the Specify slice directory name when the entry transitions to `in-progress`.

project.description: Target registry project name. Required for multi-project registries; optional for single-project (an omitted value resolves to the sole topology project). The target adapter is resolved from this project via the committed `.specify/topology.lock` (workspace) or `project.yaml.adapter` (single regular project); it is no longer stored per slice.

status.description: Current state in the per-entry status state machine. workflow collapses to three states: `pending` (written by `plan add`/`amend`), `in-progress` (written only by `plan next`), `done` (written by `plan transition <name> done`, stamped by `/spec:merge`). v1 has no per-entry `blocked`, `failed`, or `skipped` state — build failures and merge conflicts leave the active entry `in-progress`.

divergence.description: workflow §Plan-time reconciliation — closed enum for slice-level divergence state. Absent ≡ `none` (the default). `likely` is set by `/spec:plan`'s `propose` sub-step on materially-disagreeing lead synopses; `accepted` / `rejected` are set by the operator at Gate 1 via `specify plan amend --divergence`. Advisory metadata in v1. workflow §D5: every value (including `likely`) is CLI-written — the `/spec:plan` agent stages `likely` via `specify plan amend <plan> <slice> --divergence likely` (after `propose --from`), and `plan create` scaffolds an empty plan that never carries divergence.

authority-override.description: workflow §D3 — optional per-slice authority override map keyed by claim kind, valued by source key. Keys are the closed claim-kind enum (mirrored from `evidence.schema.json#/$defs/claimKind`); values MUST be source keys present in this slice's own `sources[]` list. Synthesis consults this map first when a contributing-claim disagreement remains, then falls back to the document-level Evidence `authority:` field, then to the workflow default ordering. (A per-Evidence `authority-overrides` map is deferred to a future RFC.) Empty maps and missing fields are equivalent. Orphan source keys are rejected by `specify slice validate` with `slice-authority-override-orphan-source`. CLI-written via `specify plan create --authority-override <slice> <kind>=<key>` and `specify plan amend --authority-override <slice> <kind>=<key>` / `--clear-authority-override` / `--clear-authority-overrides`.

depends-on.description: Slice names that must be `done` before this slice is eligible. Consumed by `specify plan next`.

sources.description: Per the workflow contract, an array of (source, lead) bindings. Each item is either a bare string (shorthand for `{ source: <key>, lead: <slice.name> }`) or the structured `{ source, lead }` object. Absent or `[]` → degenerate intent slice.

context.description: Baseline paths relevant to this slice, relative to `.specify/`. Briefs use these as a focus hint when scanning baseline directories.

description.description: Free-text scoping context. Carries file-path hints and references to prior slice names for delta-target inference.
